Title: Rice yellow stunt virus activates polyamine biosynthesis to promote viral propagation in insect vectors by disrupting ornithine decarboxylase antienzyme function

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: facilitates rice yellow stunt virus propagation in insect vectors
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leafhopper cells; central nervous system-associated infected tissues inferred
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm when expressed alone; recruited to nucleus with ODC1; forms cytoplasmic puncta with RYSV M
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression decreased at transcript and protein levels at 4 and 6 dpi after RYSV infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid; GST pull-down; baculovirus expression and immunofluorescence in Sf9 cells; RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; Western blot; UPLC-3Q-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ornithine decarboxylase antizyme; C-terminal ODC-binding domain (114–211 aa); produced by ribosomal frameshifting
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OAZ1 is a negative regulator of putrescine biosynthesis in N. cincticeps. 2. RYSV infection reduces OAZ1 transcript and protein accumulation. 3. RNAi knockdown of OAZ1 increases ODC1 abundance, elevates putrescine levels, and promotes RYSV accumulation and spread in the CNS.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OAZ1 is not a pathway enzyme or TF; it functions as an antienzyme controlling ODC1 degradation. RYSV M and ODC1 bind the same ODC-binding domain of OAZ1, implying competitive disruption of antizyme function.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduction of OAZ1 promotes putrescine accumulation and enhances viral infection
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq for leafhopper gene cloning/identification; accession NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antiviral target in insect vector control by maintaining or enhancing OAZ1 function to suppress polyamine-supported viral propagation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect normal polyamine homeostasis, cell growth, and insect physiology; specific tradeoffs not experimentally resolved

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: facilitates rice yellow stunt virus propagation in insect vectors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leafhopper cells; central nervous system-associated infected tissues inferred
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first rate-limiting enzyme in putrescine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased ODC1 expression elevates putrescine biosynthesis and promotes RYSV infection; ODC1 knockdown reduces putrescine and inhibits viral infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ODC1 is localized in the nucleus in Sf9 cells; its abundance is negatively controlled by OAZ1, likely via ubiquitin-independent degradation as discussed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RYSV infection; dsODC1 RNAi knockdown; DFMO treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for antiviral suppression in vectors; chemical inhibition with DFMO effectively lowers polyamines and viral load
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript and protein levels increased at 4 and 6 dpi during RYSV infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; DFMO inhibitor assay; RT-qPCR; Western blot; UPLC-3Q-MS; yeast two-hybrid; subcellular localization in Sf9 cells
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq for leafhopper gene cloning/identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Polyamine depletion may impair normal cellular growth and insect physiology; detailed fitness costs not fully evaluated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ODC1 is the first rate-limiting enzyme for putrescine biosynthesis in N. cincticeps. 2. RYSV infection increases ODC1 transcript and protein accumulation. 3. RNAi knockdown of ODC1 reduces putrescine content and restricts RYSV accumulation and CNS spread. 4. Pharmacological inhibition of ODC1 activity by DFMO reduces polyamine levels and viral propagation.
